title: Basic Profit Percentage
description: An article is bought for $600 and sold for $750. What is the profit percentage?
explanation:
Cost Price (CP) = $600
Selling Price (SP) = $750
Profit = SP - CP = 750 - 600 = $150
Profit Percentage = (Profit / CP) * 100
= (150 / 600) * 100
= (1/4) * 100 = 25%.

title: Equivalent Transactions
description: A man sells two cycles for $990 each. On one he gains 10% and on the other he loses 10%. What is his overall gain or loss percentage?
explanation:
When two items are sold at the same selling price, one at a gain of x% and other at a loss of x%, there is always a loss in the transaction.
Loss % = (x/10)^2
Here, x = 10.
Loss % = (10/10)^2 = 1^2 = 1% loss.
Alternative way:
CP of first cycle = 990 / 1.1 = $900.
CP of second cycle = 990 / 0.9 = $1100.
Total CP = 900 + 1100 = $2000.
Total SP = 990 + 990 = $1980.
Loss = 2000 - 1980 = $20.
Loss % = (20 / 2000) * 100 = 1% loss.

title: Marked Price and Discount
description: A trader marks his goods 30% above the cost price and allows a discount of 15% on the marked price. What is his gain percentage?
explanation:
Let Cost Price (CP) = $100.
Marked Price (MP) = CP + 30% of CP = $130.
Discount = 15% of MP = 0.15 * 130 = $19.5.
Selling Price (SP) = MP - Discount = 130 - 19.5 = $110.5.
Gain = SP - CP = 110.5 - 100 = $10.5.
Gain % = (10.5 / 100) * 100 = 10.5%.
Shortcut formula: Net change = x + y + (x*y)/100 = 30 + (-15) + (30 * -15)/100 = 15 - 4.5 = 10.5%.

title: Dishonest Dealer
description: A dishonest dealer claims to sell his goods at cost price, but uses a false weight of 900 grams instead of 1 kilogram. Find his actual profit percentage.
explanation:
Let the CP of 1g of goods be $1.
Then, the CP of 1000g (1kg) = $1000.
He sells 900g, but claims it is 1000g.
So, the SP of 900g = CP of 1000g = $1000.
But the CP of the 900g actually given = $900.
Profit made = SP - CP = 1000 - 900 = $100.
Profit % = (Profit / CP) * 100 = (100 / 900) * 100 = 100/9 = 11.11%.
Shortcut formula: Profit % = [Error / (True Value - Error)] * 100 = [100 / (1000 - 100)] * 100 = (100/900) * 100 = 11.11%.

title: Successive Transactions
description: A manufacturer sells a product to a distributor at a profit of 10%. The distributor sells it to a retailer at a profit of 20%, and the retailer sells it to a consumer for $396 at a profit of 25%. What is the cost of production for the manufacturer?
explanation:
Let the cost of production for the manufacturer be C.
SP of manufacturer = CP of distributor = C * 1.10
SP of distributor = CP of retailer = C * 1.10 * 1.20
SP of retailer = Price paid by consumer = C * 1.10 * 1.20 * 1.25
Given consumer price is $396:
C * 1.10 * 1.20 * 1.25 = 396
=> C * 1.65 = 396
=> C = 396 / 1.65 = 240.
Therefore, the cost of production is $240.
